checks.detail: 'agent-creator' is valid kebab-case, lowercase letters and hyphens only.
'agent-creator' is 13 characters, well under the 64-char limit.
Description is present with substantial content covering purpose, trigger scenarios, and alternate phrasings.
Description is approximately 280-320 characters (mix of CJK and ASCII), well under the 1024-char limit.

checks.item: description: front-loads key use cases within 250 chars
checks.detail: The core purpose ('创建 Claude Code 自定义 subagent') is front-loaded, but the first 250 chars cut off mid-sentence in the trigger scenario list. The most important functional description is within 250 chars, but some trigger phrases are truncated. Consider reordering to put the highest-value triggers earlier.

Includes both Chinese ('创建一个 agent', '新建子代理', '定义 agent', '做一个代码审查 agent') and English ('create an agent', 'new subagent', 'define agent', 'agent for X', 'set up a code reviewer', 'make a debugging agent') phrasings.

Description covers trigger scenarios well but lacks proactive language like 'Use proactively when...' or 'Trigger automatically when...'. It only lists scenarios passively ('当用户想要...'). Adding proactive trigger instructions would improve auto-invocation rates.

No critical fields are missing. The skill is a knowledge/task hybrid that should be both user-invocable and model-invocable (defaults). No need for disable-model-invocation, context:fork, or allowed-tools since this skill generates files as part of the main conversation.
SKILL.md is 218 lines, well under the 500-line limit. Good token efficiency.

Excellent structure: concept intro (What is Subagent) -> file format -> storage -> creation workflow (4 steps) -> type reference table -> core fields -> advanced features -> best practices -> guidelines. Progressive disclosure is well-implemented.

SKILL.md correctly delegates detailed content to examples/ and references/. The type table links to example files. Advanced fields reference frontmatter-reference.md. Inline YAML snippets are minimal and illustrative, not exhaustive.

4-step workflow covers: (1) requirements gathering with 5 key questions, (2) type selection with reference table, (3) file generation, (4) validation and deployment with specific instructions. Complete and actionable.

Missing coverage for: (1) what to do when user requirements don't match any template type (partially addressed with 'start from code-reviewer'), (2) naming conflicts between project and user-level agents, (3) debugging a non-working agent. These are minor gaps.
Clearly distinguishes subagent (single session, results return to main context) from Agent Teams (independent sessions, inter-communication). Good conceptual clarity.
Covers: '创建一个 agent', '新建子代理', '定义 agent', '做一个代码审查 agent', plus general terms 'agent 配置', 'agent YAML'.
Covers: 'create an agent', 'new subagent', 'define agent', 'agent for X', 'set up a code reviewer', 'make a debugging agent'.

Missing some common alternate phrasings: 'make a bot', 'build an assistant', 'set up an AI helper', 'configure agent permissions', 'agent template', 'create a workflow'. Also missing Chinese variants like '搭建助手', '构建机器人', '配置权限'. These could cause trigger misses for users who don't use the exact 'agent/subagent' terminology.
Description includes '构建多 agent 工作流' which covers the multi-agent coordination use case.
Includes design rationale (3 bullet points), complete agent definition with valid YAML frontmatter, and usage examples (both auto-delegation and manual). Well-structured.
Includes design rationale, complete agent definition, usage examples, and a variant section showing memory-enabled version. Excellent — demonstrates how to extend a base template.

Includes design rationale, complete agent definition with valid YAML, usage examples. Good methodology section (analyze, hypothesize, verify). Missing model field defaults to inherit, which is noted in design rationale.
Includes design rationale, complete agent definition, usage examples, and a background-mode variant. Good test discovery flow and result reporting format.

Excellent example: includes agent definition with hooks, a companion validation script with full bash code, usage examples, and a 'key takeaways' section explaining the hooks pattern is transferable. Best example for demonstrating advanced features.

Includes design rationale, complete agent definition with Agent(type) syntax, usage examples (CLI and settings.json), and important caveats (subagent limitation, token cost, Agent Teams alternative). Good coverage of edge cases.

All examples embed the agent definition inside a markdown code block (```markdown ... ```), which is the correct format for a reference example. However, the YAML frontmatter in researcher.md has the 'description' field as a multi-line string without the '>' or '|' indicator — it appears as a single line that wraps. This is technically valid YAML but may confuse users who copy-paste it if line breaks are introduced. All other examples use '>' correctly.

6 examples cover: read-only (researcher), review (code-reviewer), fix (debugger), test (test-runner), data (db-reader), coordination (coordinator). This spans the full spectrum from simple to complex, read-only to full-access.
Every example includes a 'Design Notes' section explaining tool choices, model selection, trigger strategy, and use cases. This teaches users the reasoning, not just the template.

Covers 16+ fields across 12 sections: name, description, model, effort, maxTurns, tools, disallowedTools, permissionMode, hooks, memory, mcpServers, skills, background, isolation, color, initialPrompt. Comprehensive coverage of all documented agent frontmatter fields.

Field descriptions, types, defaults, and allowed values are consistent with Claude Code documentation. Permission mode options, model resolution priority, tool control rules, and memory scoping are all accurately described.
Well-organized with a numbered table of contents (12 sections), consistent table formatting for each field group, and clear section boundaries with horizontal rules. Easy to navigate.
Provides YAML examples for: hooks (PreToolUse and PostToolUse), MCP servers (inline and reference), field combination patterns (7 different patterns). Complex fields are well-illustrated.
344 lines — substantial but appropriate for a complete field reference. Not loaded by default; only loaded on demand when specific field details are needed.
Written in Chinese (zh-CN) as required by project CLAUDE.md. All section headers, descriptions, table content, and instructional text are in Chinese.
All 6 example files are consistently written in Chinese (zh-CN). Section headers, design rationale, agent system prompts, and usage examples are all in Chinese.
frontmatter-reference.md is written in Chinese (zh-CN). All table headers, field descriptions, section titles, and explanatory text are in Chinese.

English phrases in the description field and within agent definitions ('Use proactively when...') are intentional for bilingual trigger support and are appropriate — they help the model trigger correctly regardless of the user's language.
Follows the expected skill structure: SKILL.md (main doc), examples/ (6 reference examples), references/ (field reference). Clean separation of concerns.
SKILL.md links to examples/ files (6 links in the type table) and references/frontmatter-reference.md (2 links). All referenced files exist in the directory.
SKILL.md provides a summary table of core fields; frontmatter-reference.md provides the complete reference. Examples demonstrate specific patterns. No significant content duplication detected.

recommendations: TRIGGER IMPROVEMENT: Add more edge-case trigger phrasings to the description. Consider adding: 'build an assistant', 'make a bot', 'agent template', 'configure agent permissions', '搭建助手', '配置权限'. This would improve trigger accuracy for users who don't use 'agent/subagent' terminology.

DESCRIPTION PUSHINESS: Add proactive trigger language to the description. Currently it uses passive framing ('当用户想要...'). Adding 'Use proactively when user discusses creating automated workflows or specialized AI assistants' would increase auto-invocation rates.

DESCRIPTION FRONT-LOADING: Reorganize the description so the most distinctive trigger phrases appear within the first 250 characters. Currently the core purpose is front-loaded well, but some English triggers may be truncated from the skill list display.

EDGE CASE COVERAGE: Consider adding a brief troubleshooting section or FAQ to SKILL.md covering: (1) what to do if an agent doesn't trigger, (2) naming conflict resolution between project and user-level agents, (3) how to debug a malfunctioning agent. This would add maybe 15-20 lines.

RESEARCHER EXAMPLE YAML: In researcher.md, the description field in the agent definition code block appears as a single long line without the YAML '>' multi-line indicator. While technically valid, adding '>' would be more consistent with the other examples and more robust for copy-paste usage.
